---
title: "Analysis of Factors Affecting Well-Being"
author: "Vladyslav Rudenko"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.08
    footer: "Well-Being Analysis — Vladyslav Rudenko"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

plt.rcParams.update({
    "figure.facecolor": "white",
    "axes.facecolor": "#FAFAFA",
    "axes.edgecolor": "#D0D0D0",
    "axes.linewidth": 1.0,
    "axes.grid": True,
    "axes.axisbelow": True,
    "grid.color": "#E6E6E6",
    "grid.linestyle": "--",
    "axes.titlesize": 13,
    "axes.titleweight": "bold",
    "axes.labelsize": 11,
    "xtick.labelsize": 9,
    "ytick.labelsize": 9,
})

_ID = {"person_id", "employee_id"}

def numeric_features(df, target):
    num = df.select_dtypes(include="number").columns.tolist()
    return [c for c in num if c != target and c not in _ID]

def target_correlations(df, target):
    feats = numeric_features(df, target)
    return df[feats + [target]].corr(numeric_only=True)[target].drop(target)

def plot_corr_slide(df, target, title):
    corr = target_correlations(df, target).sort_values()
    colors = ["#B65E5A" if v < 0 else "#4C78A8" for v in corr.values]
    h = max(4.0, len(corr) * 0.5)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, h))
    ax.barh(corr.index, corr.values, color=colors, edgecolor="none")
    ax.axvline(0, color="#444", linewidth=1)
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=14)
    ax.set_xlabel("Pearson r")
    for i, v in enumerate(corr.values):
        ha = "left" if v >= 0 else "right"
        ax.text(v + (0.012 if v >= 0 else -0.012), i, f"{v:.2f}",
                va="center", ha=ha, fontsize=9)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    plt.close(fig)

DATA_DIR = "data/clean_data"
df_pca      = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/clean_pca.csv")
df_prod     = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/clean_productivity.csv")
df_sleep    = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/clean_sleep.csv")
df_students = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/clean_students.csv")
df_remote   = pd.read_csv(f"{DATA_DIR}/clean_remote.csv")
```

## Project Idea

I analysed which factors are related to well-being, stress, and productivity.

The main focus was on:

- Sleep
- Stress
- Physical activity
- Daily habits
- Productivity and work-life balance

---

## Datasets

| Dataset | Target |
|---|---|
| Lifestyle | wellness |
| Productivity | productivity |
| Sleep | stress_level |
| Students | stress_numeric |
| Remote work | productivity_score |

I analysed the datasets separately because they describe different groups of people.

---

## Methods

I used two main methods:

**Correlation analysis**
Shows which variables are directly related to the target.

**PCA**
Shows the main structure of the dataset and reduces many variables into
several principal components.

---

## Lifestyle Dataset

Target: **wellness**

```{python}
plot_corr_slide(df_pca, "wellness", "Lifestyle: Correlation with Wellness")
```

This plot shows which lifestyle factors are most related to the wellness score.

---

## Productivity Dataset

Target: **productivity**

```{python}
plot_corr_slide(df_prod, "productivity", "Productivity: Correlation with Productivity")
```

This helped identify which daily habits are connected with higher or lower productivity.

---

## Sleep Dataset

Target: **stress_level**

```{python}
plot_corr_slide(df_sleep, "stress_level", "Sleep: Correlation with Stress Level")
```

This dataset was useful because stress is strongly connected with sleep and health variables.

---

## Student Dataset

Target: **stress_numeric**

```{python}
plot_corr_slide(df_students, "stress_numeric", "Students: Correlation with Stress")
```

The goal was to see how student lifestyle factors are related to stress.

---

## Remote Work Dataset

Target: **productivity_score**

```{python}
plot_corr_slide(df_remote, "productivity_score", "Remote Work: Correlation with Productivity Score")
```

This part focused on productivity in remote work conditions.

---

## Conclusion

The same types of variables appeared repeatedly:

- **Sleep** (quality and duration)
- **Stress**
- **Physical activity**
- **Daily habits**
- **Lifestyle or work-life balance**

The analysis does not prove causality, but it shows clear statistical
relationships in the available data.
